$schema-note: Fixture corpus for ilb-llm-tokens v1.0. Each fixture pairs a V1 input shape with the equivalent V2 shape conveying the same lint finding. Edit here, not in source code.

v2.why: Attackers can modify the query structure to access unauthorized data
v2.vulnerable: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=' + userId)

v1.fix: Read secret from environment variable or secret manager

v2.why: Secrets in code leak via repos, logs, and crash dumps
v2.fix: Read secret from environment variable or secret manager

v1.description: JWT verified with algorithm 'none' bypasses signature check

v2.description: JWT verified with algorithm 'none' bypasses signature check
v2.why: Anyone can forge a token by setting alg=none in the header

v1.description: MongoDB $where executes arbitrary JavaScript on the server
v1.fix: Replace $where with $expr or specific query operators

v2.description: MongoDB $where executes arbitrary JavaScript on the server
v2.why: String inputs concatenated into $where give attackers code execution
v2.fix: Replace $where with $expr or specific query operators

v1.description: Untrusted input written to innerHTML enables XSS
v1.fix: Use textContent, or sanitize with DOMPurify before assignment

v2.description: Untrusted input written to innerHTML enables XSS
v2.why: innerHTML parses script tags and event handlers from the input
v2.fix: Use textContent, or sanitize with DOMPurify before assignment

v1.description: MD5 is broken for cryptographic use
v1.fix: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 for hashes; use bcrypt/argon2 for passwords

v2.description: MD5 is broken for cryptographic use
v2.why: MD5 collisions are trivially generatable; not safe for integrity or auth
v2.fix: Use SHA-256 or SHA-3 for hashes; use bcrypt/argon2 for passwords

v1.description: Circular import detected: A → B → C → A
v1.fix: Extract shared types to a separate module

v2.description: Circular import detected: A → B → C → A
v2.why: Circular imports cause undefined values at import time
v2.fix: Extract shared types to a separate module

v1.fix: Import from the source module directly

v2.why: Bundlers retain all transitive members when a barrel is in the import chain
v2.fix: Import from the source module directly

v1.description: Loop-based INSERT detected — O(n) database calls
v1.fix: Use bulk INSERT with a single VALUES clause

v2.description: Loop-based INSERT detected — O(n) database calls
v2.why: Each iteration opens a new round-trip, causing N×latency
v2.fix: Use bulk INSERT with a single VALUES clause
v2.vulnerable: for (const row of rows) { await db.query("INSERT...", row); }
v2.safe: await db.query("INSERT INTO t VALUES " + values.join(","), flat)

v1.description: N+1 query pattern in collection iteration
v1.fix: Eager-load related records with a single JOIN or batch lookup

v2.description: N+1 query pattern in collection iteration
v2.why: Each row triggers an additional fetch, multiplying database load
v2.fix: Eager-load related records with a single JOIN or batch lookup

v1.description: Array.push inside a recursive call without termination guard
v1.fix: Add a max depth or size guard

v2.description: Array.push inside a recursive call without termination guard
v2.why: Memory grows linearly with input size; risk of OOM on adversarial input
v2.fix: Add a max depth or size guard
v2.safe: walk(node, depth=0) { if (depth > MAX) return; acc.push(node); node.children.forEach(c => walk(c, depth+1)); }
